sit: Two attackers entered your zone and both D picked them up. A third attacker trails in 5 seconds later — nobody picked them up. You're the center on defense. What do you do?
opts: Stay with your current assignment and trust that someone else will pick the third up
Pick up the late attacker — your winger on that side needs to find their assignment
Call it out loudly so a D on the other side can come over to cover
Ignore the trailer and let your D partner figure the coverage out

sit: You're the offensive zone D. The puck went to the corner. Your winger got there first and has possession, but a defender is closing. No coverage behind you. Your other winger is at the half-wall. Should you pinch?
opts: Yes — pinch down to the corner and help your winger win the battle for the puck
No — your winger has possession and your half-wall winger can support. Hold the blue line.
Yes — any time a teammate is battling for the puck you should pinch down
Skate to the net-front instead

sit: You're the point on the power play. You have the puck and a shooting lane — but two PK forwards are closing on you hard. You have one second. What's the best play?
opts: Shoot the puck immediately before the forwards close the lane on you — any shot is a chance
Fake the shot to freeze them, then pass to the half-wall player who opened up when they committed to you
Hold onto the puck and wait for a cleaner shooting lane to open up on its own
Pass it back to the other point

sit: You're forechecking. The D picks up the puck behind the net and starts to walk out to the left. You're closing from the right. Your linemate is on the left. How do you split?
opts: Both skate straight at the D
You force the D's direction from the right, your linemate cuts off the outlet to the left — trap them between you
You back off to the high slot and let your linemate handle the forecheck on their own
Both pull back and reset

sit: Your team is forechecking and the other team wins a battle in the corner. They're about to break out with speed. You're the high forward. What's the most important thing you do right now?
opts: Chase the puck into the corner
Hold your position and intercept the first outlet pass — you're the plug
Skate back hard to your defensive zone right away
Go to the bench

sit: Your team just won a puck battle in your own zone. Two of your forwards are already at center ice. The other team has one player back. A 3-on-1 is developing. What's the trigger?
opts: Wait until all three forwards are lined up across the neutral zone together
Go immediately — the 3-on-1 window closes fast as the other team gets back
Regroup at your own blue line first
Dump the puck deep and change lines

sit: Your team breaks out. As you cross the red line you see both opposing defensemen are pinching up — they're at the red line, not their blue line. What do you do?
opts: Slow down and let them set up their defense — forcing the play into two pinching D is risky, so wait for your team to get on side behind you
Skate hard behind them — you have a breakaway if you beat them to their blue line
Dump the puck in deep — chip it past both D and chase because they're pinching up too high to recover on a dump-in behind them
Pass across to a winger on the far side — switch the play to the weak side where there's more open ice to attack the zone together

sit: You're protecting the puck behind the net with a defender leaning on you. Two teammates are in the zone but both are covered. What do you do?
opts: Force a pass to a covered teammate — a hard crisp pass through traffic can break coverage if you put it on the tape before a defender reacts
Wheel to the other side of the net — moving forces the defender to move and may open a teammate
Try to power through the defender — use your lower body to drive through the check and carry the puck out in front of the net to shoot
Fall down to draw a penalty call — go to the ice hard and look at the ref so a hooking or holding call gets called on the defender leaning on you

sit: You have the puck in the corner. Two defenders are collapsing on you from different angles. Your only open teammate is at the blue line — a long pass blocked by two defenders. What do you do?
opts: Attempt the long stretch pass to the blue line and hope it sneaks through the two defenders
Protect the puck tight to the boards with your body, wait for the angle to open, then make a short chip pass
Try to spin through both defenders with quick footwork and create your own lane out
Shoot the puck hard around the boards and trust a teammate is there on the other side

sit: A skilled attacker gets a step on you at the blue line and is now skating toward your net with speed. You can't catch them. What's the right approach?
opts: Give up the chase and skate calmly back so you're set for the next rush after the scoring chance
Get your stick into their lane — angle them toward the boards and get your body in front of the shooting lane
Try to grab them
Dive forward and slide on your belly to block the shot — your body can take away the whole lane

sit: You're the defenseman and an attacker is coming in at speed. They fake to the backhand. You start to move with the fake. What do you do next?
opts: Keep moving with the fake and commit hard in that direction
Stop your movement, reset your feet, and regain your gap — don't fully commit
Drop and block the shot
Poke check right away

sit: Your power play is in a 1-3-1. The half-wall player has the puck. The near PK forward is cheating toward the half-wall. The seam pass to the middle player is now open. What's the right play?
opts: Hold the puck at the half-wall and wait
Thread the seam pass to the middle player — the PK forward cheating toward you opened that
Shoot from the half-wall — any shot on net with a PP advantage is a good one
Pass the puck back to the point and let them try to shoot from up high

sit: Your penalty kill gets the puck out of your zone. It's a 2-on-3 as you exit. Do you try to score shorthanded or just clear?
opts: Go for the shorthanded goal — attack the net
Clear the zone and get back — a clean clear is a win on the PK
Pass back to your goalie
Dump it back in

sit: You're about to receive a pass in the offensive zone. Before the puck reaches you, what should you already be doing?
opts: Standing still with your stick on the ice so the passer has an easy target to aim for
Looking up and deciding where you're going with the puck before it arrives — pre-reading the play
Calling loudly for the puck so the passer knows exactly where you are on the ice
Watching the puck all the way onto your blade so you don't fumble the reception

sit: You're cycling below the dots. Your partner passes to you at the half-wall. The slot is completely empty. What's the play?
opts: Keep cycling — pass back down to the corner and try again on the next rotation
Shoot immediately into the open slot or pass to a teammate breaking there
Hold the puck
Drop the puck back to the point defenseman

sit: You're at the half-wall with the puck. The point defender is cheating toward the net. The slot defender is watching the corner. Nobody is tight on you. What's the play?
opts: Pass the puck down to the corner — find a teammate low behind the goal line to start a cycle with the defender cheating up toward the net instead
Shoot from the half-wall — you have time and the slot defender is distracted
Pass up to the point defender — move the puck to the blue line for a one-timer since you have time and the slot defender isn't watching you
Drop the puck behind you immediately — leave it for a trailing teammate to pick up since nobody is pressuring you on the half-wall right now

sit: Two forecheckers are deep in your zone. Your winger is up the wall but covered. Your center in the middle is also covered. Your D has the puck behind the net. What's the best option?
opts: Force a pass to a covered teammate — a quick hard pass can break through the coverage if you put it on the tape before the forechecker reacts
Wheel and carry it out — both forecheckers are committed to your forwards. The D carrying it out is the best option.
Shoot the puck around the boards — rim it hard along the wall past the forecheckers and let your winger chase it up to the neutral zone
Wait behind the net with the puck — hold it until the forecheckers commit to one side and then make your decision based on what they do

sit: You're on a clean 1-on-1 rush. The defenseman is the only one back. They're skating backward. What's the best approach?
opts: Skate straight at the defender full speed and try to blow by them wide
Skate with control, read their skates, and make a move when they commit
Shoot right away from the far blue line before the defender can close off
Slow way down and wait for help

sit: You're the left defenseman. Your partner calls switch as an attacker cuts from the right side to the left in front of the net. What do you do?
opts: Stay with the attacker you started with — never switch assignments in your own zone
Take the attacker coming to your side, let your partner clean up their original man
Call for the referee
Back up to the crease

sit: Your team is in the offensive zone. The other team won a puck battle and is trying to break out. You're the nearest forward. What's the most effective way to maintain pressure?
opts: Chase the puck carrier at top speed into corner — full pressure on the puck forces a bad pass and keeps the offensive zone pressure alive for your team
Read their breakout pass options and cut them off — be in the lane before the pass is
Give up on the play and change lines — you're already tired from the forecheck, so get to the bench and let fresh legs take over the shift
Back off to the blue line and reset — let them break out clean and set up your neutral zone defense to stop them at the red line with the D

sit: Your team is winning by one goal with 90 seconds left. You win a puck battle in the neutral zone. What's the right thing to do with the puck?
opts: Attack immediately — try to score another goal so the lead becomes safer with only 90 seconds left
Control the puck along the boards, protect it, and force the other team to come get it — use every second
Dump the puck deep into the other team's zone and forecheck hard to keep it pinned down there
Shoot it the length of the ice

sit: You receive a pass in the slot. The goalie is way out of their net, cutting the angle aggressively. Where should you shoot?
opts: High to the blocker side — beat them over the shoulder
Low and to one side — an aggressive goalie is exposed low
Straight at them
Deke around the goalie

sit: Your team is on the penalty kill. You win the puck along the boards in your own zone. What's the priority?
opts: Pass the puck around your zone until a perfect breakout option opens up
Get the puck out of your zone as fast as possible — any clean clear is a win
Hold the puck in your corner to kill time off the penalty
Try to score a shorthanded goal

sit: Your team is on the power play. The penalty kill gets the puck and two players immediately break the other way 2-on-2. What should your team do?
opts: Chase both attackers hard and try to attack and score on the rush the other way
One forward pressures the puck, the others recover their positions — don't panic
All five players retreat to the front of your own net
Ignore the rush and stay in the offensive zone ready for the next chance

sit: The other team is cycling below the dots in your zone. You're the weak-side defenseman. When do you leave your position to challenge?
opts: Leave your spot every time the puck moves so you can keep pressure on whoever has it
When a player with the puck is in a dangerous spot — near the slot or about to pass to the slot
Never leave your spot — stay planted at your position
Leave only when your D partner calls for help on the puck

sit: Your team is attacking 3-on-2. The play breaks down and the other team gets the puck. You're the center, close to the play. What do you do first?
opts: Skate straight to the bench and get off the ice
Pressure the puck carrier immediately to slow the breakout
Skate back toward your own blue line to set up defensively
Stand still and watch

sit: You're low in the offensive zone with the puck. The D at the point just stepped down from the blue line because they saw a lane. They're now inside the zone at the top of the circles. What's the play?
opts: Ignore the D and pass to the corner — keep the cycle going low instead of feeding a defender who just stepped down from the point to the slot
Pass to the D who stepped in — they now have a shot from a dangerous area
Shoot from the corner yourself — a bad-angle shot can still create a rebound for a teammate and the D stepping in acts as a screen now
Pass up the boards to a winger — find a teammate along the wall to reset the play because the D stepping in leaves the point wide open

sit: Your team is breaking out. The strong-side winger is completely covered. The D picks up the puck on the right side. The center is in the middle and the left winger is open on the short side. What's the right read?
opts: Force the pass to the covered strong-side winger — the puck will sort itself out
Pass to the open left winger on the short side — shortest distance, cleanest
Carry it out yourself
Rim it around the boards

sit: The other team is on the power play. Their point man has the puck. One of their forwards is screening your goalie in front of the crease. What's your job as the nearest PK forward?
opts: Chase after the point man to pressure the shot from outside
Push the screener out of the crease area — your goalie needs to see
Stay in the passing lane
Back up toward your own goal line
